title: Museum Island Before and After WWII - Quick Summary
items: **Museum Island was not preserved untouched.** It was damaged, divided, repaired and rebuilt across decades.
**UNESCO listed Museum Island in 1999,** the same period when the modern master plan for restoration and modernization took shape.
**The Altes Museum burned out almost completely** near the end of World War II and was restored between 1958 and 1966.
**The Neues Museum stayed a ruin for more than 60 years** before reopening in 2009 after David Chipperfield's reconstruction.
**The Pergamonmuseum story is still unfolding:** the building is closed now, with a major reopening planned for 4 June 2027.
